title: Find the weakest claim in the spec
description: Read docs/spec.md and identify the single claim most likely to be false, overstated, or unprovable as written. Quote it, explain why it is weak, and state what evidence would be needed to defend it.
success_criteria: Names one concrete claim with a quote, a specific reason it is weak, and the missing evidence. 'Zero idle CPU', 'race-free', lossless backpressure, or the dependency set are all fair game.

title: Name the prior art that sinks this
description: Identify an existing project, OS feature, or paper that a hostile reviewer would use to dismiss shellmux as 'just X'. State the dismissal precisely, then judge whether docs/prior-art.md already pre-empts it adequately.
success_criteria: Names a real prior art (honker, Mosquitto/NATS, at/cron, timerfd, sleep-loop, tee-to-FIFOs), states the 'this is just X' framing, and verdicts whether prior-art.md defuses it.

title: Verify the borrowed-mechanism citations
description: Open the cited source lines under terminalphone/ and honker/ and confirm each says what the spec claims. Flag any citation that is wrong, stale, or oversold (e.g. a 'backpressure fix' that is actually a leaky pattern).
success_criteria: Checks honker_ops.rs:536-558, honker-rs/lib.rs:828/1105-1106/1558-1582, terminalphone.sh:1206/1518-1546/1567-1572/1674. Confirms the :1570 '> $f &' is correctly named as the bug, not the fix.

title: State the exact race window the chaos test must hit
description: From the spec/design, describe in precise terms the window between deadline-computation and the blocking wait, and explain how the chaos harness must inject a publish into it to be a valid proof.
success_criteria: Identifies the window as 'after next=MIN(run_at), before read -t entered', describes a concrete instrumentation hook to pause there, and notes 5000+ trials with jittered timing.

title: Design the must-fail negative control
description: Propose the deliberately-broken scheduler variant the chaos harness must also run, and explain exactly why it should produce missed or duplicate fires. Without this, the proof is unfalsifiable.
success_criteria: Describes a broken variant (poke-then-stage, or sleep-without-rescan) and a clear causal story for why it misses/dups, plus the assertion that the harness catches it.

title: Build the bash/timer platform matrix
description: Across macOS bash 3.2, dash, and Linux bash>=4, state which features (fractional read -t, flock, timeout, socat) are present and what timer resolution results. Identify where a judge would get a bad surprise.
success_criteria: Produces a matrix showing ~1s resolution on bash3/dash vs sub-100ms on bash>=4, missing flock/timeout on bare macOS, and cites honker's from_secs to justify the ~1s floor as faithful.

title: Publish reaches all live subscribers, nobody else
description: Start the broker. Subscribe two clients to a topic, leave a third on a different topic. Publish one message. Confirm exactly the two reach it and the third does not.
success_criteria: Both same-topic subscribers receive the message intact; the off-topic subscriber receives nothing; no error output.

title: A --delay message lands on time at ~0% idle CPU
description: Run 'shellmux pub control --delay 5 reboot' and verify it arrives ~5s later while 'top' shows ~0% scheduler CPU for the whole interval.
success_criteria: Message arrives within the documented resolution of the deadline; a top/ps sample during the wait shows ~0% scheduler CPU. Latency within idle_poll worst case.

title: Run the deadline chaos test for 5000 trials
description: Execute tests/chaos_deadline.sh and confirm it reports missed=0 dup=0 over N>=5000 adversarial-timing trials, AND that its negative-control broken variant is shown to fail.
success_criteria: Correct variant: missed=0 dup=0 over >=5000 trials. Negative control: missed>0 or dup>0 (the broken variant is caught). Both conditions required.

title: Flood a wedged subscriber; prove writers don't accumulate
description: With three subscribers (one wedged via 'socat ... | (read x; sleep 999)'), flood the topic. Confirm the two healthy subscribers stay real-time, drops_<wedged> rises, and 'ps --ppid $PUB | wc -l' stays flat over time.
success_criteria: Healthy subscribers keep receiving in real time; cat drops_<wedged> increases; background-process/fd count stays flat (no per-message process leak); publisher rate stays flat.

title: Inspect the whole broker with ls and cat only
description: Without any admin command, enumerate topics, count subscribers per topic, read drop counters, and list pending deferred messages using only ls and cat.
success_criteria: ls topics/, ls topics/$T/sub_*.fifo | wc -l, cat topics/$T/drops_*, ls topics/$T/deferred | sort all produce sensible output; no bespoke CLI needed.

title: Feed malformed and adversarial payloads
description: Publish payloads with embedded newlines, NUL bytes, multi-KB size, a wrong/oversized length prefix, and bogus --at values (past, far future, non-numeric). Confirm no crash, no hang, and that length-prefix framing discards torn frames rather than concatenating.
success_criteria: Binary/newline/NUL payloads deliver intact under length-prefix framing; a deliberately wrong length prefix yields a discarded frame (short-read), not corruption; bogus --at is rejected or handled without a stuck/forever-firing state; broker stays up.

title: Deferred messages survive a broker crash
description: Schedule a --delay message, kill -9 the broker before it fires, restart, and confirm the message still fires after restart (documented at-most-once-modulo-crash).
success_criteria: Deferred file survives the kill; scheduler re-arms from deferred/ on restart; the message fires (at most one duplicate only if crash landed between mv and delivery).

title: Measure the real subscriber/fd ceiling on a Pi
description: Open subscribers until something breaks (fd/process limit), on the actual demo hardware. Record the measured ceiling rather than the rounded 'hundreds'.
success_criteria: Produces a concrete measured number of concurrent subscribers before fd/process limits bite on the Pi, with the limiting resource named (ulimit -n, pid_max, etc.).

title: Probe for at-least-once / ack semantics (beyond scope)
description: Attempt to get a delivery guarantee: retry-until-acked, replay of missed messages for a subscriber that was briefly offline. Confirm the tool is honest that this is punted, not silently broken.
success_criteria: The tool/docs make clear there is no at-least-once, no replay, no persistence beyond deferred files; the user is not misled into thinking missed messages are recoverable.
